# Code of Conduct

## Our Standard

This project should be a respectful, practical place to collaborate. Be kind,
specific, and constructive when discussing code, results, limitations, or design
tradeoffs.

Expected behavior:

- Use welcoming and inclusive language.
- Respect different skill levels and backgrounds.
- Give feedback that focuses on the work, not the person.
- Acknowledge uncertainty when discussing benchmark results.
- Treat security, privacy, and data quality concerns seriously.

Unacceptable behavior:

- Harassment, insults, threats, or discriminatory language.
- Publishing private information without permission.
- Deliberately misrepresenting benchmark results or model capabilities.
- Disruptive comments that prevent productive technical discussion.

## Enforcement

Project maintainers may edit, hide, or remove comments, issues, pull requests,
or other contributions that violate these expectations. Repeated or severe
violations may result in a temporary or permanent ban from project spaces.

## Reporting

If you see behavior that violates this code, open a private security advisory
or contact the repository owner through GitHub. Include links, screenshots, or
other context when possible.
